'''George Lawler''' (born 1 September 1995) is an English professional rugby league footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr|sr|lf}} or {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League, and has spent time on dual registration from Hull KR at [[Newcastle Thunder]] and [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Lawler was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], Humberside, England.

==Playing career==
===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Lawler is a former West Hull amateur, George was a product of and another player that blossomed through the ranks of the City of Hull Academy, before he started spending more time with the Hull Kingston Rovers' first-team squad.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

On 23 August, 2015, he made a try scoring début for Hull Kingston Rovers against the [[Widnes Vikings]] in an 8–12 victory in [[The Qualifiers]].

It was revealed on 2 March 2016, that Lawler had signed a four-year contract at Hull Kingston Rovers following an impressive start to his professional playing career.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Lawler suffered relegation from the [[Super League]] with Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2016 season, due to losing the [[Million Pound Game]] at the hands of the [[Salford Red Devils]] in golden point extra time.

12-months later, however, Lawler was part of a Hull Kingston Rovers side that won promotion back to the Super League, at the first time of asking following relegation the season prior.

It was announced on 4 May 2018, that Lawler had penned a new three-year contract extension, to remain at Hull Kingston Rovers until at least the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44011819 |title=George Lawler: Hull KR back-rower signs three-year contract extension |work=BBC Sport |date=4 May 2018 |access-date=14 August 2024}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
On 5 October 2021, it was announced that Lawler would sign for the [[Castleford Tigers]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-10-05 |title=George Lawler: Castleford Tigers sign Hull KR back-rower |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58800845 |access-date=2022-03-27 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=Sky Sports|date=5 Oct 2021|accessdate=16 Oct 2021|title=George Lawler: Castleford Tigers clinch signing of Hull KR forward for 2022 Super League season|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12426461/george-lawler-castleford-tigers-clinch-signing-of-hull-kr-forward-for-2022-super-league-season}}</ref> For the [[2022 Castleford Tigers season|2022 season]], he claimed squad number 10 which was vacated by the retiring [[Grant Millington]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-01-21 |title=Tigers reveal 2022 squad numbers |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7841 |access-date=2022-03-27 |website=Castleford Tigers |archive-date=5 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220205022008/https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7841 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

Lawler made his Castleford debut on 11 February 2022 in the opening round fixture against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-02-11 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16-26 Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60320580 |access-date=2022-03-27 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored his first try for the club on 26 March against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[2022 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-03-26 |title=Challenge Cup: Castleford Tigers demolish Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60850397 |access-date=2022-03-27 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> During his first season he made twenty-three appearances, featuring predominantly as a {{Rlp|pr}} or {{Rlp|sr}} forward, but also filling in at {{Rlp|hk}}.<ref name="RLRKC" />

Lawler scored his second Tigers try against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] on 10 March 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 March 2023 |title=Super League: Huddersfield Giants 36-6 Castleford Tigers - Coachless Cas beaten |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64907128 |access-date=7 July 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In June, he sustained a [[Quadriceps|quad]] injury which kept him out for seven weeks.<ref>{{Cite web |date=13 June 2023 |title=Tigers player and injury update |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8635 |access-date=7 July 2024 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He played twenty matches in total for Castleford in the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]] as the club narrowly avoided relegation by finishing above [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]]. In October, he signed a two-year contract extension with Cas.<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 October 2023 |title=George Lawler: Castleford Tigers back-rower extends contract |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67060134 |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 3 April 2024, Lawler was hospitalised following a seizure at his home during the night.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=5 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers player George Lawler in hospital after scans reveal bleed on his brain |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-player-george-lawler-in-hospital-after-scans-reveal-bleed-on-his-brain-4580191 |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=4 April 2024 |title=George Lawler: Castleford Tigers prop in hospital with brain bleed |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68732467 |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Initial scans indicated a small [[Brain bleed|bleed on the brain]] and he remained in hospital for further testing and specialist involvement, with former teammate [[Jamie Peacock]] writing, "everyone in the rugby league world will be wishing him the best in his recovery."<ref>{{Cite web |date=6 April 2024 |title=Jamie Peacock: RL world behind Lawler |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/4101/jamie-peacock-rl-world-behind-lawler |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=Super League}}</ref> After three months of recovery and phased return to training, Lawler returned to the team for Castleford's victory against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] on 5 July,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=28 May 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' ex-Hull KR star George Lawler set for return to training after bleed on brain |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-ex-hull-kr-star-george-lawler-set-for-return-to-training-after-bleed-on-brain-4644225 |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=20 July 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' Joe Westerman hails George Lawler's comeback from "scary" ordeal |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-joe-westerman-george-lawler-33273485 |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=The Mirror}}</ref> and was named in the ''Love Rugby League'' team of the week.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=8 July 2024 |title=Wigan Warriors, Castleford Tigers provide 7 players in Super League Team of the Week |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-castleford-tigers-in-super-league-team-of-the-week |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> In September, Castleford confirmed that neurological reviews had verified there was no brain injury suffered, and Lawler finished the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|season]] having made fifteen appearances.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=23 September 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers give update on George Lawler following seizure earlier in the year |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-give-update-on-george-lawler-following-seizure-earlier-in-the-year/ |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

Lawler was appointed Castleford vice-captain ahead of the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=14 December 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' leadership group confirmed as Danny McGuire makes surprise captaincy call |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-captains-danny-mcguire-sam-wood |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He scored two tries in round 13 against [[Warrington Wolves]] and was named in the team of the week.<ref>{{Cite web |date=30 May 2025 |title=Wire tee up Wembley date with win against Cas |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czx0pwdlv78o |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2 June 2025 |title=Round 13 Team of the Week |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/5117/round-13-team-of-the-week |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=Super League}}</ref> In July, he scored a try in three consecutive rounds, against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and Warrington, and was selected in the round 19 team of the week.<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 July 2025 |title=Round 19 Team of the Week |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/5248/round-19-team-of-the-week |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=Super League}}</ref> On 23 July, he signed a new three-year deal with the club; director of rugby [[Chris Chester (rugby league)|Chris Chester]] said, "George is a very influential player in the squad and consistently performs well. He's a player that leads with his actions on the field and is equally impressive off it."<ref>{{Cite web |date=23 July 2025 |title=Lawler signs new three-year deal at Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ceq74wqvyvzo |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=24 July 2025 |title=Chris Chester delighted with new deal for 'influential' George Lawler |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/chris-chester-delighted-with-new-deal-for-influential-george-lawler-5239793 |access-date=17 May 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> He was ever-present throughout the year, featuring in all twenty-eight matches for Castleford as the club finished 11th in the Super League.<ref name="RLRKC" />

In round 13 of the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]], Lawler made his 100th Castleford appearance and marked the occasion with a try.<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 June 2026 |title=Cook try gives Leigh victory over Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cg5pjlzz43vo |access-date=10 June 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
